insights: Incident distribution across the five categories (Database, Hardware, Inquiry / Help, Network, Software) is perfectly uniform, with exactly 100 incidents per category and a Gini coefficient of 0.0000, indicating zero structural imbalance in category assignment.

Assignee workload is perfectly balanced across all five agents (Beth Anglin, Charlie Whitherspoon, Fred Luddy, Howard Johnson, Luke Wilson), each handling exactly 100 incidents—eliminating any evidence of individual overloading or underutilization.

Assignment group distribution mirrors category and assignee patterns, with each of the five groups (Database, Hardware, Inquiry / Help, Network, Software) receiving exactly 100 incidents, confirming systemic uniformity in routing logic or assignment policy.
The absence of imbalance across all three core dimensions—category, assignee, and assignment group—suggests intentional design or synthetic data generation rather than organic operational variation.

Despite high overall data quality (no missing values, consistent schema), 52.8% of incidents exhibit negative resolution times, revealing a critical timestamp integrity issue that undermines reliability of time-based metrics but does not affect categorical distribution conclusions.
summary: The analysis definitively confirms there is no discrepancy or imbalance in incident assignment across categories—the core business goal—nor across assignees or assignment groups: all five categories, five assignees, and five assignment groups each receive exactly 100 incidents (20% share), yielding a Gini coefficient of 0.0000 in every case. This perfect uniformity indicates either a deliberately controlled assignment mechanism or a synthetically constructed dataset, not real-world operational variance. While categorical and workload distributions are ideal from a balance perspective, the pervasive timestamp inconsistency (52.8% negative resolution times) poses a serious risk to any time-sensitive operational analysis, such as SLA compliance or performance benchmarking. Next focus should prioritize root cause investigation of the timestamp inversion issue before proceeding with time-dependent diagnostics or forecasting.
